data.body: SCOPE DECISION (during implementation): the same-tracker race test surfaced a real TOCTOU bug in the parent-cascade (src/rebar/_commands/claim.py): the cascade decides to claim the parent on an UNLOCKED stale 'open' read; under a genuine race the second agent's locked parent claim raises ConcurrencyMismatch and the whole CHILD claim aborts with exit 10 -- so 'both children end claimed' (this story's AC) and the documented single-agent contract ('parent already in_progress -> only the requested ticket moves') are BOTH violated. Fixing claim.py in scope (adds it to file_impact): when the cascaded parent claim fails but the parent is no longer open (a peer progressed it), that is benign -> proceed to claim the child; only a still-open parent aborts. This makes the AC true and aligns the race with documented intent. RED-before-GREEN: corrected same-tracker test authored to assert both children claimed (RED on current code), then fix.
